annotations.label: Definition of 9

anchor.snippet: is the natural number following 8 and preceding 10

annotations.note: The natural number 9 is available as the numeric literal `(9 : ℕ)` via Mathlib's standard numeric infrastructure, with no dedicated definition needed.

annotations.label: 9 as composite, square, Motzkin, derangement count
annotations.note: Mathlib has no notion of `Nat.Composite` nor a Motzkin-numbers definition, and no lemma identifying 9 as the 4th composite, a square, a Motzkin number, or the derangement count of 4.

annotations.label: Divisibility rule for 9

anchor.snippet: A decimal integer is divisible by 9 if and only if the sum of its digits is divisible by 9

annotations.note: `Nat.nine_dvd_iff` states `9 ∣ n ↔ 9 ∣ (digits 10 n).sum`, exactly the divisibility-by-9 rule.

annotations.label: 9 as sum of two consecutive positive cubes

anchor.snippet: 9 is the only square number that is the sum of two consecutive, positive cubes

annotations.note: No grep hits for a theorem about squares that are sums of two consecutive positive cubes.

annotations.label: Odd perfect number prime factor bound

anchor.snippet: If an odd perfect number exists, it will have at least nine distinct prime factors

annotations.note: No `OddPerfect` declaration or lower-bound results on odd perfect numbers' prime factors exist in Mathlib.

annotations.label: 9 as first cube-sum number greater than one

anchor.snippet: which makes it the first cube-sum number greater than one

annotations.note: Mathlib has no definition of `cube-sum number` nor a result identifying 9 as the first such number above 1.

annotations.label: Sum of three cubes obstruction mod 9

anchor.snippet: A number that is 4 or 5 modulo 9 cannot be represented as the sum of three cubes

annotations.note: No grep hits for the sum-of-three-cubes mod 9 obstruction in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Nine Heegner numbers
annotations.note: Heegner numbers appear only in a comment in `Mathlib/Analysis/Real/Pi/Chudnovsky.lean`; no formal definition or finiteness theorem exists.

annotations.label: 9 largest single-digit decimal number

anchor.snippet: 9 is the largest single-digit number in the decimal system

annotations.note: This meta-statement about decimal notation is not formalized as a theorem in Mathlib.

annotations.label: Nine dots puzzle

anchor.snippet: The nine dots puzzle is a mathematical puzzle whose task is to connect nine squarely arranged points

annotations.note: The nine dots puzzle has no Mathlib formalization.

annotations.label: Nonagon

anchor.snippet: A polygon with nine sides is called an nonagon or occasionally, an enneagon

annotations.note: Mathlib has no named definition `Nonagon`/`Enneagon` for 9-sided polygons.

annotations.label: Constructibility of the regular nonagon

anchor.snippet: A regular nonagon can be constructed with a regular compass

annotations.note: Mathlib lacks a Gauss–Wantzel-style constructibility classification of regular polygons and any nonagon-specific result.

annotations.label: Minimum squares for perfect rectangle tiling

anchor.snippet: The lowest number of squares needed for a perfect tiling of a rectangle is 9

annotations.note: No Mathlib results on squared/perfect rectangle tilings or their minimum order were found.
